steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Juchipila is a striking sight for soldiers as they view it from afar. The correct quotation must satisfy two criteria: (1) the soldiers are at a distance, not inside or moving through the town, and (2) the town appears visually striking.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "All day long [the soldiers] rode through the canyon, up and down the steep, round hills, dirty and bald as a man's head, hill after hill in endless succession.": Describes the soldiers' journey through terrain, not a distant view of Juchipila. The town is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — "[The soldiers] entered the streets of Juchipila as the church bells rang, loud and joyfully, with that peculiar tone that thrills every mountaineer.": The soldiers are inside Juchipila, not viewing it from afar. ✗
  • Choice C — "Juchipila arose in the distance, white, bathed in sunlight, shining in the midst of a thick forest at the foot of a proud, lofty mountain.": "Arose in the distance" establishes the remote viewing angle. "White, bathed in sunlight, shining" conveys the striking visual impression. Both criteria are met. ✓
  • Choice D — "The sierra is clad in gala colors. Over its inaccessible peaks the opalescent fog settles like a snowy veil on the forehead of a bride.": Describes the surrounding mountain scenery, not Juchipila itself being viewed from afar. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
